description_long: This sculpture depicts a reclining bearded male figure, possibly a river god or mythological character, with a muscular physique and draped cloth over his lower body. He is accompanied by a smaller child figure, who sits at his side and appears to be interacting with the main figure. The adult figure's right arm is bent and rests on his knee, while his left arm is extended behind him, holding an object. The child is positioned near the adult's legs, with one arm reaching forward. The figures are set on a rectangular plinth with detailed surface textures, including flowing drapery and organic elements. The entire sculpture has a weathered, metallic appearance, suggesting it is cast in bronze or a similar material.
description: A detailed, reclining male figure with a child companion, both rendered in a classical style and set on a rectangular plinth. The sculpture features intricate drapery and lifelike anatomy, with a metallic, weathered finish.
description_view_0: The front view shows the bearded male figure reclining with his right arm resting on his knee and the child figure seated at his side, both clearly visible.
description_view_1: The rear view highlights the muscular back of the adult figure and the draped cloth, with the child figure partially obscured.
description_view_2: The left side view displays the length of the adult figure's body and the child figure's profile, emphasizing the interaction between them.
description_view_3: The right side view presents the adult figure's chest and the child figure in a more forward-facing position, with both figures' faces partially visible.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; the sculpture is isolated on a plain background.
